Common Questions

Garage Door Repair FAQs

How much does garage door spring repair cost in Phoenix? ▼
$150–$350 depending on spring type, door weight, and number of springs. Speedy Check Garage Doors provides free on-site estimates. Call (480) 480-7303.
How long does a spring replacement take? ▼
1–2 hours. In most cases a technician can be at your Phoenix-area home the same day you call, with the repair completed in a single visit.
Can I use my garage door with a broken spring? ▼
No. Springs counterbalance 150–300 lbs of door weight. Using the opener without a functioning spring can burn out the motor or cause the door to fall. Call immediately.
Does Speedy Check offer 24/7 emergency service? ▼
Yes. 24/7 emergency garage door repair throughout Phoenix, Scottsdale, Chandler, Gilbert, Peoria, Glendale, Sun City West, and Ahwatukee. Call (480) 480-7303 any time.
Is Speedy Check licensed in Arizona? ▼
Yes. Arizona ROC License #362309. Fully licensed, bonded, and insured — family-owned in the Phoenix metro area.

🔧 Garage Door Spring Repair — Phoenix, AZ

Speedy Check Garage Doors provides same-day spring repair in Phoenix, AZ. We replace torsion and extension springs 24/7.

A broken spring is the #1 reason a garage door won't open. Call (480) 480-7303.

ServiceSpring Repair
LocationPhoenix, AZ & Metro
Availability24/7, Same-Day
Phone(480) 480-7303

Signs Your Spring Is Broken

  • Door won't open or feels extremely heavy to lift manually
  • Opener runs but door barely moves
  • Loud bang heard from the garage
  • Visible gap in the spring coil above the door
  • One side of the door higher than the other
  • Cables hanging loose on the sides

Torsion vs. Extension Springs

FeatureTorsionExtension
LocationAbove door, on a shaftAlong side tracks
Lifespan15,000–20,000 cycles5,000–10,000 cycles
Common inModern homesOlder homes
DIY safe?NoNo

Spring Broken? Don't Wait.

Same-day spring repair in Phoenix, AZ. We answer 24/7.

(480) 480-7303


Spring Repair FAQs

Common questions about spring repair in Phoenix, AZ.

How much does spring repair cost in Phoenix? ▼
$150–$350 depending on spring type and door weight. Free on-site estimates before any work begins.
Should I replace one spring or both? ▼
If one breaks, the other is likely near end-of-life too. Replacing both saves a second service call and ensures balanced operation.
Can a broken spring damage my opener? ▼
Yes. Running the opener with a broken spring forces it to lift 150–300 lbs unassisted, which can burn out the motor. Stop using it immediately.

ServiceOpener & Motor Repair
BrandsLiftMaster, Chamberlain, Genie
Availability24/7, Same-Day
Phone(480) 480-7303

Common Problems We Fix

  • Opener runs but door doesn't move
  • Remote or keypad not responding
  • Safety sensors blinking or misaligned
  • Grinding or straining sounds during operation
  • Door reverses before fully closing
  • Wall button works but remote doesn't

Repair or Replace?

If your opener is more than 10–15 years old, or repair costs exceed 50% of replacement cost, a new opener is often the smarter investment. We'll give you an honest assessment — no upsell pressure.

Opener Not Working?

Same-day opener repair in Phoenix. All brands. Free estimate.

(480) 480-7303


Opener Repair FAQs

Common questions about opener repair in Phoenix, AZ.

Why does my opener run but not move the door? ▼
Most likely a broken spring. Stop using the opener immediately — running it without spring assistance can burn out the motor.
Why is my sensor light blinking? ▼
The sensors are misaligned, obstructed, or dirty. They need a clear line of sight. If cleaning and realigning doesn't fix it, wiring or the logic board may need service.
How long do garage door openers last? ▼
10–15 years with normal use. Phoenix's extreme heat can shorten rubber belt life. Past 15 years, replacement is usually more cost-effective.

IncludesOld door removal
Phone(480) 480-7303

Signs It's Time to Replace

  • Structural damage — dents, cracks, or warping that can't be repaired
  • Repeated repairs adding up to near replacement cost
  • Poor insulation — major issue with Phoenix summers
  • Outdated appearance hurting curb appeal or resale value
SituationAction
Single broken spring or opener issueRepair
Structural damage or bent frameReplace
Door 20+ years old, multiple issuesReplace
Poor insulation in Phoenix heatReplace with insulated door

Replacement FAQs

Common questions about door replacement in Phoenix, AZ.

How much does a new garage door cost in Phoenix? ▼
$700–$2,500 installed depending on size, material, insulation, and style. Free on-site estimates — call (480) 480-7303.
Best garage door for Phoenix heat? ▼
An insulated steel door with R-12 to R-18 rating. Clopay and Amarr both offer strong options built for desert climates.
